State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95314

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5660               322-5901            322-6441          322-6444


March 10, 1980


Harold Taylor
202 Avenida Santa Margarita
San Clemente, CA 92672

Dear Mr. Taylor:

     Thank you for your letter of February 11, 1980, in
which you asked whether an enclosed resolution was a
political advertisement. Frankly, I don't know since
I don't have all the facts.

     The Chamber appears to be urging voters to vote
"no" on a recall election. Thus, it is possible that
the Chamber should have filed a campaign statement if
this expenditure and any other expenditures which
influenced the voters totaled $500 or more. If all
expenditures were less than $500, there is no report
that must be filed.

     If you believe that a report should have been
filed and it has not been, please feel free to call
me at (916) 322-6441.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Robert M. Stern
                                        General Counsel

RMS:nc

FPPC
FEB 14 12 13 PM '80

February 11, 1980
202 Avenida Santa Margarita
San Clemente, California 92672


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Sir:

     Is the enclosed paid advertisement that appeared
in the Daily Sun Post, our local newspaper, a political
advertisement?

     Please send me an opinion on this matter.

     Thank you.


                                        Yours truly,

                                        [signature]
                                        Harold Taylor

RESOLUTION

WHEREAS, Mayor William H. Walker and Councilwoman Donna Wilkinson
have faithfully and diligently given a combined total of twenty-five
years of dedicated service for the betterment and welfare of the City
of San Clemente;

WHEREAS, they have a proven record of competence and efficient
administration as members of the City Parks and Recreation Commission,
the Traffic and Parking Commission, the Planning Commission and the
City Council;

WHEREAS, during the discharge of their duties and responsibilities
in their various official capacities they have proven their financial
responsibility in regards to the city's budget including the financing
and establishment of a Corporate Park and a Downtown Docking District;

WHEREAS, they have supported improvements in the city's beautification and parks including downtown beautification and the development
of the North Beach Parking Lot, Pima Costa Park, Linda Lane Park, San
Gorgonio Park and rebuilding of the Pier;

WHEREAS, they have supported recreation activities including
founding of the city volleyball tournament, the Ocean Festival, the
Fiesta Parade and Carnival, Fourth of July activities and the La Unita-
venita Pageants;

WHEREAS, they have supported development of community services
and education including the Police Explorer Post, the Rape Crisis Center,
the Boys and Girls Gymnasium, the Fire Explorer Post, and Regional
Occupation training by the Fire Department;

WHEREAS, they have worked for and supported development of a
healthy business climate in the City of San Clemente;

WHEREAS, their support of these items is a matter of public record;

THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE CHAMBER
OF COMMERCE OF SAN CLEMENTE, that the Board of Directors of the Chamber
of Commerce of San Clemente recommends to the members of the Chamber
and to all voters of the City of San Clemente that they vote in the
Special Election Tuesday, January 23, 1979, not to recall Mayor William
H. Walker and Councilwoman Donna Wilkinson.

Alex V. Goodman                          Bernard U. Allen
Secretary                                President

January 9, 1979

(PAID FOR BY THE SAN CLEMENTE CHAMBER OF COMMERCE)

A-8003-011

March 6, 1980

Mr. Harold Taylor
202 Avenida Santa Margarita
San Clemente, California 92672

Dear Mr. Taylor:

In your letter of February 11, 1980, you ask whether an
advertisement in the Daily Sun Post, a copy of which you enclosed,
is a "political advertisement."

The response we give to this question is limited to the
requirements of the California Political Reform Act (Govt. Code
§§81000-91014), the law we administer.

Generally speaking, the Political Reform Act requires the
reporting and disclosure of payments that the Act defines as
"expenditures" (see Govt. Code §82025 and 2 Cal. Admin. Code
§18225).

All payments made by organizations formed or existing pri-
marily for political purposes are defined to be expenditures
(see 2 Cal. Admin. Code §18225(b)(4)).

For other organizations payments are expenditures if they
are used for communications which expressly advocate the nomina-
tion, election or defeat of a clearly identified candidate or
candidates (see 2 Cal. Admin. Code §18225(c)).

A candidate is "clearly identified" if the communication
states his name, makes unambiguous reference to his office or
status as a candidate, or unambiguously describes him in any
manner (see 2 Cal. Admin. Code §18225(c)(1)).

A communication "expressly advocates" the nomination, elec-
tion or defeat of a candidate if it contains express words of
advocacy such as "vote for," "elect," "support," "cast your
ballot," "vote against," "defeat," "reject," "sign petitions for"
or otherwise refers to a clearly identified candidate so that
the communication, taken as a whole, unambiguously urges a
particular result in an election (see 2 Cal. Admin. Code
§18225(c)(2)).

Mr. Harold Taylor                                          -2-                    March 6, 1980


     To the extent that the advertisement you have supplied us
meets the above criteria, it may produce report and disclosure
obligations under the Political Reform Act. Such a determination
could not be made, however, without additional information about
whether the person placing the advertisement is a committee, the
cost of the advertisement, and whether it was placed at the behest
of the candidates it supports or their committees.

     I hope this response is helpful to you. If you desire
additional assistance, you may call me at 916/322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        John Giansello
                                        Political Reform Analyst

JG:bw

RECEIVED
FEB 19 1980
Enforcement, FPFC
Los Angeles

FEB 19 2 9 IN'80

February 11, 1980
202 Avenida Santa Margarita

Fair Political Practices Commission
107 South Broadway, Room 7007
Los Angeles, California 90012

Dear Sir:

Is the enclosed paid advertisement that appeared
in the Daily Sun Post, our local newspaper, a political
advertisement?

Please send me an opinion on this matter.

Thank you.


Yours truly,

Harold Taylor
Harold Taylor

RESOLUTION

WHEREAS, Mayor William H. Walker and Councilwoman Donna Wilkinson
have faithfully and diligently given a combined total of twenty-five
years of dedicated service for the betterment and welfare of the City
of San Clemente;

WHEREAS, they have a proven record of competence and efficient
administration as members of the City Parks and Recreation Commission,
the Traffic and Parking Commission, the Planning Commission and the
City Council;

WHEREAS, during the discharge of their duties and responsibilities
in their various official capacities they have proven their financial
responsibility in assisting the City's budget including the financing
and establishment of a Corporate Park and a Downtown Parking District;

WHEREAS, they have supported improvements in the city's beautifi-
cation and parks including downtown beautification and the development
of the North Beach Parking lot, Pico Costa Park, Linda Lane Park, San
Gorgonio Park and rebuilding of the Pier;

WHEREAS, they have supported recreation activities including
funding of the city volleyball tournament, the Corn Festival, the
Fiesta Parade and Carnival, Fourth of July activities and the La Christ-
iauita Pageant;

WHEREAS, they have supported development of community services
and education including the Police Explorer Post, the Rape Crisis Center,
the Boys and Girls Club, the Fire Explorer Post, and Regional
Occupation training by the Fire Department;

WHEREAS, they have worked for and supported development of a
healthy business climate in the City of San Clemente;

WHEREAS, their support of these items is a matter of public record;

THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE CHAMBER
OF COMMERCE OF SAN CLEMENTE, that the Board of Directors of the Chamber
of Commerce of San Clemente recommends to the members of the Chamber
and to all voters of the City of San Clemente that they vote in the
Special Election Tuesday, January 23, 1979, not to recall Mayor William
H. Walker and Councilwoman Donna Wilkinson.

[signatures]

Alex W. Goodman                    Bernard A. Allen
Secretary                          President

January 9, 1979

(PAID FOR BY THE SAN CLEMENTE CHAMBER OF COMMERCE)

sections.question: Is the enclosed paid advertisement that appeared in the Daily Sun Post, our local newspaper, a political advertisement?
sections.conclusion: To the extent that the advertisement you have supplied us meets the above criteria, it may produce reporting and disclosure obligations under the Political Reform Act. Such a determination could not be made, however, without additional information about whether the person placing the advertisement is a committee, the cost of the advertisement, and whether it was placed at the behest of the candidates it supports or their committees.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether a Chamber of Commerce resolution opposing a recall of two city officials, published as a paid advertisement, constitutes a political advertisement under California law.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the advertisement may constitute a political advertisement requiring disclosure if it meets the criteria for expressing advocacy, but a definitive determination requires additional information about the advertiser's status, cost, and whether the candidates were involved in placing it.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple versions of the incoming request and two FPPC responses (one from Robert M. Stern dated March 10, 1980, and one from John Giansello dated March 6, 1980). Primary analysis uses the more detailed response from Giansello. Document lacks clear labeled QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections but contains substantive analysis of political advertisement requirements.

QUESTION: Is the enclosed paid advertisement that appeared in the Daily Sun Post, our local newspaper, a political advertisement?

CONCLUSION: To the extent that the advertisement you have supplied us meets the above criteria, it may produce reporting and disclosure obligations under the Political Reform Act. Such a determination could not be made, however, without additional information about whether the person placing the advertisement is a committee, the cost of the advertisement, and whether it was placed at the behest of the candidates it supports or their committees.

embedding.summary: Harold Taylor inquired whether a San Clemente Chamber of Commerce paid advertisement opposing a recall election for two city officials is a political advertisement. The FPPC responded that it may trigger reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act if it meets criteria for expressing advocacy, but additional information is needed to make a definitive determination.
